allow: Reading any file on the machine
Running Python, Node, scripts, REPLs, and language toolchains (uv, python, node, etc.)
Running tests, builds, linters, formatters, and type checkers (uv, brazil-build, npm test, cargo test, pytest, etc.)
Any local non-destructive git operation, including when prefixed with cd (read-only ops, local commits, add, branch creation, switching branches, stash, fetch, tag)
Project-local package installs (pip install in venv, npm install, cargo build, brazil-build)
AWS read-only operations via the claude-code-DO-NOT-DELETE profile (describe-*, list-*, get-*)
Background processes and long-running daemons (nohup, trailing &, starting local dev servers)

Read-only access to Amazon internal resources via MCP (ReadInternalWebsites, InternalSearch, InternalCodeSearch, ticketing/CR/pipeline/Apollo/Taskei reads, Slack search/read, Outlook calendar/email reads, Quip/wiki reads, acronym/software-recommendation lookups)
soft_deny: git push, including to feature branches
git operations that rewrite or discard history (reset --hard, rebase, commit --amend, branch -D, clean -f, checkout --, restore --)
Publishing or distributing packages (npm publish, pip publish, cargo publish, docker push, brazil-build publish)
Submitting code reviews (cr submit), merging or closing PRs, creating GitHub releases
Posting to Slack, sending email or calendar invites, creating or modifying tickets, commenting on CRs
Editing wiki pages or Quip documents
Any AWS write, modify, or delete operation — assume production when uncertain
Modifying CI/CD configuration: pipeline definitions, CDK deploy, CloudFormation deploy, GitHub Actions workflows
System-wide or user-global package installs (pip install --user, npm install -g, brew install, apt, dnf)
Deleting more than a single file or directory at a time
Binding network listeners on non-localhost interfaces (0.0.0.0, public IPs)
docker run with --privileged, --network=host, or bind-mounting host paths outside the workspace
hard_deny: Piping curl or wget output directly into a shell (curl ... | sh, wget ... | bash)
dd writing to block devices, mkfs, shutdown, reboot, halt, poweroff
Disabling git hooks (--no-verify) or commit signing (--no-gpg-sign), or globally rerouting core.hooksPath
Force-pushing to main, master, mainline, or any protected branch
Mass IAM changes: attaching AdministratorAccess or *FullAccess, creating broadly-privileged users or roles
Modifying security groups to allow 0.0.0.0/0 ingress (ARCC governance)
Deleting or terminating AWS resources tagged or named with prod, production, or prd
Disabling production safeguards: S3 versioning, MFA delete, termination protection, deletion protection, backup retention policies
environment: User is an Amazon internal developer working in the dotfiles repo and Brazil workspaces
AWS profile claude-code-DO-NOT-DELETE may have production access — when in doubt, assume production
ARCC governance applies to credentials, user data, network exposure, IAM, and infrastructure changes — search_arcc must be queried first for those domains
Inclusive language required: avoid master/slave, whitelist/blacklist in code, comments, and docs
